title: hieracium lachenalii subsp. macrophyllopodum
intro: Hieracium lachenalii subsp. macrophyllopodum, also known as the spotted hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Hieracium lachenalii subsp. macrophyllopodum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal properties.
